question: [File: 'File Name: web3/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/web3/Web3Properties.java -> Scope: High: Preventing gossip of a transaction or multiple transactions'] [Function: maxPayloadLogSize] Can an unprivileged user send a request with a Content-Type that causes the ContentCachingRequestWrapper to misparse the body, resulting in the transaction payload being silently dropped and never gossiped to the network?

[File: 'File Name: web3/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/web3/Web3Properties.java -> Scope: High: Preventing gossip of a transaction or multiple transactions'] [Function: maxPayloadLogSize] Can an unprivileged user send a chunked-transfer-encoded request body that exceeds maxPayloadLogSize * 10 bytes, causing the ContentCachingRequestWrapper to buffer only the first maxPayloadLogSize * 10 bytes and silently discard the rest, resulting in a truncated transaction that is never gossiped?

[File: 'File Name: web3/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/web3/Web3Properties.java -> Scope: High: Preventing gossip of a transaction or multiple transactions'] [Function: requestTimeout] Can an unprivileged user send a request that causes the web3 node to open a large number of database cursors within the requestTimeout window, exhausting the database connection pool and preventing other transaction-processing queries from executing, thus blocking gossip?

[File: 'File Name: web3/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/web3/Web3Properties.java -> Scope: High: Preventing gossip of a transaction or multiple transactions'] [Function: requestTimeout] Can an unprivileged user craft a contract call that triggers recursive EVM execution (e.g., via CALL/DELEGATECALL loops) that issues no SQL statements, bypassing the statementInspector timeout entirely, running for longer than requestTimeout and monopolizing a thread needed for transaction gossip?

[File: 'File Name: web3/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/web3/Web3Properties.java -> Scope: High: Preventing gossip of a transaction or multiple transactions'] [Function: requestTimeout] Can an unprivileged user send a request that causes the web3 node to enter an infinite retry loop in the gas estimation algorithm (maxGasEstimateRetriesCount=20), where each retry resets the ContractCallContext startTime, effectively resetting the requestTimeout clock and allowing the request to run indefinitely, blocking gossip?

[File: 'File Name: web3/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/web3/Web3Properties.java -> Scope: High: Preventing gossip of a transaction or multiple transactions'] [Function: maxPayloadLogSize] Can an unprivileged user send a request with a payload containing a crafted 'data' field value that causes the DATA_PATTERN regex reorderFields() to produce output longer than the input, bypassing the maxPayloadLogSize truncation and writing unbounded data to logs, filling disk and preventing transaction gossip?

[File: 'File Name: web3/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/web3/Web3Properties.java -> Scope: High: Preventing gossip of a transaction or multiple transactions'] [Function: requestTimeout] Can an unprivileged user exploit the absence of a global rate limit on the number of concurrent requests to send thousands of simultaneous requests each consuming the full requestTimeout (10 seconds), exhausting all available threads and preventing any transaction from being gossiped during that window?

[File: 'File Name: web3/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/web3/Web3Properties.java -> Scope: High: Preventing gossip of a transaction or multiple transactions'] [Function: requestTimeout] Can an unprivileged user send a request that causes the statementInspector to be invoked with a null ContractCallContext (e.g., by triggering a code path that clears the context mid-execution), causing a NullPointerException that crashes the request handler thread and prevents gossip of the associated transaction?

[File: 'File Name: web3/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/web3/Web3Properties.java -> Scope: High: Preventing gossip of a transaction or multiple transactions'] [Function: requestTimeout] Can an unprivileged user craft a request that causes the web3 node to issue a single extremely long-running SQL query (e.g., a full table scan triggered by a crafted contract address), which the statementInspector only checks at the start of the query (not during execution), allowing the query to run past requestTimeout and block gossip?

[File: 'File Name: web3/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/web3/Web3Properties.java -> Scope: High: Preventing gossip of a transaction or multiple transactions'] [Function: maxPayloadLogSize] Can an unprivileged user send a request with a payload that, after GZIP compression in getContent(), produces a Base64-encoded string that is exactly maxPayloadLogSize characters, causing the compressed content to be logged in full, and by sending many such requests simultaneously, fill the log buffer and cause log4j/logback to block, stalling transaction processing and gossip?

[File: 'File Name: web3/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/web3/Web3Properties.java -> Scope: High: Preventing gossip of a transaction or multiple transactions'] [Function: requestTimeout] Can an unprivileged user send a request that triggers a cache miss storm (e.g., by querying many unique contract addresses), causing the web3 node to issue a large number of database queries each just under the requestTimeout threshold, collectively exhausting DB connections and preventing gossip-related transaction queries?

[File: 'File Name: web3/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/web3/Web3Properties.java -> Scope: High: Preventing gossip of a transaction or multiple transactions'] [Function: requestTimeout] Can an unprivileged user exploit the fact that the statementInspector timeout check uses wall-clock time (System.currentTimeMillis()) rather than CPU time, allowing a request to survive a system clock adjustment or NTP sync that moves time backward, resetting the elapsed time and bypassing the timeout, blocking gossip indefinitely?

[File: 'File Name: web3/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/web3/Web3Properties.java -> Scope: High: Preventing gossip of a transaction or multiple transactions'] [Function: requestTimeout] Can an unprivileged user send a request that causes the web3 node to allocate large amounts of memory during EVM execution (e.g., via MSTORE/MLOAD with large offsets), triggering GC pauses that extend the effective execution time beyond requestTimeout without the statementInspector firing (since no SQL is issued during GC), blocking gossip?

[File: 'File Name: web3/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/web3/Web3Properties.java -> Scope: High: Preventing gossip of a transaction or multiple transactions'] [Function: requestTimeout] Can an unprivileged user send a request that causes the web3 node to enter a long-running contract simulation that issues SQL queries at exactly the requestTimeout boundary, causing the statementInspector to throw QueryTimeoutException on every query, generating a flood of exceptions that overwhelms the error handler and prevents gossip?

[File: 'File Name: web3/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/web3/Web3Properties.java -> Scope: High: Preventing gossip of a transaction or multiple transactions'] [Function: requestTimeout] Can an unprivileged user send a request that causes the web3 node to open a WebSocket or long-poll connection that holds a thread for the full requestTimeout duration, and by opening many such connections simultaneously, exhaust the thread pool and prevent legitimate transactions from being gossiped?

[File: 'File Name: web3/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/web3/Web3Properties.java -> Scope: High: Preventing gossip of a transaction or multiple transactions'] [Function: requestTimeout] Can an unprivileged user craft a contract call that triggers a precompile execution path that does not issue any SQL statements, bypassing the statementInspector timeout check entirely, running indefinitely and blocking a thread needed for transaction gossip?

[File: 'File Name: web3/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/web3/Web3Properties.java -> Scope: High: Preventing gossip of a transaction or multiple transactions'] [Function: requestTimeout] Can an unprivileged user send a request that causes the web3 node to acquire a database lock that is never released within the requestTimeout window, causing subsequent transaction-processing queries to block waiting for the lock and preventing gossip of those transactions?

[File: 'File Name: web3/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/web3/Web3Properties.java -> Scope: High: Preventing gossip of a transaction or multiple transactions'] [Function: maxPayloadLogSize] Can an unprivileged user send a multipart request with many small parts that individually stay under maxPayloadLogSize but collectively exhaust the ContentCachingRequestWrapper buffer, causing the server to allocate excessive memory and preventing transaction gossip by triggering OOM?

[File: 'File Name: web3/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/web3/Web3Properties.java -> Scope: High: Preventing gossip of a transaction or multiple transactions'] [Function: requestTimeout] Can an unprivileged user send a request that causes the web3 node to perform a large number of cache lookups (e.g., by querying a contract with many storage slots), each triggering a DB query just under the requestTimeout threshold, collectively exhausting the DB connection pool and preventing gossip-related queries from executing?

[File: 'File Name: web3/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/web3/Web3Properties.java -> Scope: High: Preventing gossip of a transaction or multiple transactions'] [Function: requestTimeout] Can an unprivileged user exploit the fact that the requestTimeout is enforced only at the SQL layer (statementInspector) and not at the HTTP layer, by sending a request with a very large body that takes longer than requestTimeout to read, causing the server to block on I/O and never reach the SQL layer, effectively bypassing the timeout and blocking gossip?
